name: Honey Barbecued Chicken
recipeIngredient: 8 tablespoons Tarragon or white wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine honey, vinegar, curry powder, and dried tarragon.
2. Remove the grates from the barbecue.
3. Place one or two shallow drip pans filled with water or stock (you can also add a splash of white wine on the lava rocks) under the cooking grate.
4. Preheat the barbecue to high setting.
5. Arrange chickens on the spit, ensuring they are evenly balanced.
6. Brush the chickens with the honey basting sauce and place the rotisserie on the barbecue with the drip pans directly underneath.
7. Roast at high heat for 10 minutes.
8. Reduce the heat to medium and roast the chickens, with the lid closed, for about 1½ to 2 hours, basting every 30 minutes with sauce until a thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the thigh registers 185°F (85°C).
9. Remove from the barbecue, season with salt and pepper, and tent with foil.
10. Let stand for 10 minutes before carving.
11. Serves six to eight.
INDIRECT COOKING METHOD FOR GAS BARBECUES WITH TWO BURNERS:
1. Place a shallow metal or foil pan (water is not necessary) under one side of the grill to catch drippings.
2. Preheat both sides of the barbecue on high setting.
3. Tie the wings and legs of the chicken with string; brush lightly with oil.
4. Place the chicken breast-side down on the hot grill and cook for two minutes.
5. Turn the chicken on its side and grill for two minutes.
6. Turn the chicken back, and continue to grill for two more minutes until browned all over.
7. Place the chicken, breast-side up, on the cooler side of the barbecue with the drip pan underneath; turn off the burner on that side.
8. Brush liberally with sauce and roast the chicken with the lid closed for about 90 minutes, basting occasionally with sauce.
9. This method also works well with beef, pork, or lamb roasts.
